doc.title: Sell an AFS security — the deferred gain comes home
doc.event: Mona Co. sells AFS bonds carried at amortized cost $50,000 (with a $4,000 prior unrealized gain in AOCI) for $55,000. Prepare the sale entry.

lines.label: proceeds
lines.trap: As always: amortize to the sale date and settle accrued interest separately first.

lines.label: amortized cost off
lines.why: The investment clears at amortized cost — the fair-value layer lives in the adjustment account, not here.
lines.trap: Don't credit the FV-adjusted 54,000; the adjustment account unwinds separately at the next portfolio true-up.

lines.label: proceeds − amortized cost
lines.why: The FULL realized gain — including the 4,000 that had been deferred in OCI — lands in net income at sale.
lines.trap: Realized gain measures against amortized COST, not against last year-end's fair value. The previously-parked OCI recycles rather than reducing the gain.

computationPaths.narration: The recycling story: 4,000 of this gain was already reported in OCI over prior periods. At sale it reclassifies OUT of AOCI and INTO net income (via the portfolio's next fair-value true-up and the comprehensive-income reclassification adjustment) — total comprehensive income over the security's life counts the gain exactly once.

steps.label: Reclassification adjustment
steps.formulaText: 4,000 leaves AOCI so it isn't double-counted

memorize.body: AFS sale: realized G/L = proceeds − amortized cost, all to income. Prior unrealized amounts in AOCI reclassify out (the 'reclassification adjustment') so comprehensive income never counts the same gain twice.
The Fair Value Adjustment account is a PORTFOLIO-level allowance — it re-targets at the next year-end for whatever securities remain; it isn't reversed security-by-security at each sale.

questions.prompt: AFS bonds: amortized cost 50,000, prior unrealized gain in AOCI 4,000, sold for 55,000. Gain reported in NET INCOME at sale =

distractors.feedback: 1,000 nets the sale against last year's FAIR VALUE (54,000) — but that 4,000 was only ever in OCI, never in income. At sale the whole 5,000 (proceeds − amortized cost) hits income, and the 4,000 recycles out of AOCI.

accountBank.account: Unrealized Holding Gain or Loss — Equity

build.scaffold: The sale entry itself is plain: cash, investment off at amortized cost, realized plug. The OCI unwind happens at the portfolio true-up — resist stuffing it into this entry.
